reason: Response A has the distinctive Haiku texture: short declarative sentences, sharp reframes ("That feeling is information, not a verdict"), and a willingness to name the genuinely hard, slightly uncomfortable truth (giving yourself permission to be a beginner) without lapsing into stock motivational phrasing. Response B reads as a polished imitation, leaning heavily on familiar inspirational cliches ("Don't compare your beginning to someone else's middle," "compounds," "You've got this") and smoother but more generic balanced paragraphs typical of a larger model performing warmth.
